title: Asukas fatherland
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's fatherland             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Jan 27, 2014 1:51 am Was it ever said what part of Germany Asuka comes from? I have a blurry memory, possibly false, of a doujin which said it was Bavaria, even though that doesn't mean anything obviously.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 27, 2014 2:05 am IIRC NERV Germany is labelled as being in Hamburg in the displays in EoE; so we'd expect that she'd have spent most of her life there. As to her family background and birthplace, the series (and supplemental material) is silent. You can always fanwank about the Zeppelin connection. Related -- http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/4244/What-is-Asukas-heritage/

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jan 27, 2014 12:05 pm In my headcanon, she's totally Suebian. Redheads are more common in the south*, and she totally has the attitude. "We can do everything except speak standard German... er, read Kanji!" I can imagine her being all pround of their industrious, patent-producing reputation and lapsing into dialect when she's exasperated. *there's actually a hilarious urban legend that blames this solely on one particular emperor who had some british ancestry, as well as a bit of a womanizing reputation.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Jan 27, 2014 1:32 pm Yeah she is way too feisty to be from Northern Germany. They are known for their reserved and cool-headed nature.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Mon Jan 27, 2014 3:24 pm Fireball wrote: Yeah she is way too feisty to be from Northern Germany. They are known for their reserved and cool-headed nature.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Jan 27, 2014 3:27 pm driftking18594 wrote: I don't think a typically reserved north German would be so reserved if they had Asuka's backstory.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Mon Jan 27, 2014 3:34 pm This thread has made me realise how little I know about regional German stereotypes.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jan 27, 2014 3:37 pm driftking18594 wrote: Now I'm wondering what part of Britain Mari's from. My headcanon says somewhere near (the submerged ruins of) London.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Mon Jan 27, 2014 3:38 pm I'm asking myself if we actually can make assumptions based on cultural differences of the different regions, if we don't know how Germany looked after 2I, aside from being "badly damaged". If I had to tell, based on the current "status" I would "locate" her in the south. I don't really know why, but many girls around the age 14 here seem to be this kind of nagging, narcissistic prude. Although I could image her to be a "berliner Göre".

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 27, 2014 3:46 pm ElMariachi wrote: Arsenal! Don't ask me why, but I feel it right that she comes from around there!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jan 27, 2014 4:28 pm ^ Mh? I don't know anything about that, it's just the idea of a crazy British chick loving giant guns living near a cit called Arsenal was fitting, that's all!

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Mon Jan 27, 2014 4:34 pm Asuka goes on and on about how German she is, but she seems to align much more closely with Japanese stereotypes of Americans. Note how rude she is in her first few appearances, for example, or how often she's seen eating or near food (subtle, but it's there). Her BGM even has a country/Western flair . People often forget that she's only 1/4 German anyway, her dad being named Langley and all.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jan 27, 2014 5:34 pm But even if her father is American and therefor she's 1/2 American, she still lived in Germany and identify herself as a German rather than an American : her Eva's neural language is in German, she talks to her stepmother in German in episode 22, meaning that her father still lives in Germany...

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jan 27, 2014 5:51 pm She might live with the stepmother and not see the father at all. If you think about it, that would make a certain amount of sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 27, 2014 5:57 pm ElMariachi wrote: meaning that her father still lives in Germany...

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Mon Jan 27, 2014 6:31 pm Even though her surname already suggests a connection to the English gentry? (Of course, this could just be the creators not knowing what names are typical in the Eurosphere...)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jan 27, 2014 8:01 pm This thread puzzles me. I mean, as best we know Asuka's father was American, and she was born in Japan (Unit 02 was built in Japan, then moved to Germany for final assembly and activation). That suggests that, while Asuka certainly grew up in Germany, she isn't from Germany. She's from Japan. Or, more specifically, she's an American, with a German grandparent, who was born in Japan and raised by a German stepmother. That's about all we have to work with. As to her and her mother's foreign names, I think y'all are making too much of them. Langley's American so there's no connection to English gentry there, and it's been a long, long time since the Zeppelins had any pull in the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Mon Jan 27, 2014 8:10 pm Bagheera wrote: As to her and her mother's foreign names, I think y'all are making too much of them. Langley's American so there's no connection to English gentry there, and it's been a long, long time since the Zeppelins had any pull in the world.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jan 27, 2014 8:14 pm Bagheera wrote: I mean, as best we know Asuka's father was American, and she was born in Japan

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jan 27, 2014 8:35 pm Atropos wrote: Edmund Langley . And...where is it said Asuka was born in Japan? Seems a bit hypocritical to complain about people "making too much" of things and then going off and making an unjustified claim like that.
summary:
[]: Asuka's fatherland             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Jan 27, 2014 1:51 am Was it ever said what part of Germany Asuka comes from? I have a blurry memory, possibly false, of a doujin which said it was Bavaria, even though that doesn't mean anything obviously.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 27, 2014 2:05 am IIRC NERV Germany is labelled as being in Hamb
